description:
The daemon binds to 127.0.0.1:0 with zero authentication. Any local process can send JSON commands to the port advertised in .beads/obelisk.port to spawn agents, kill agents, or stop the daemon. This is the single biggest security gap — any local user or compromised process has full control of the orchestrator.

Recommendation: Add a file-based shared secret (e.g., write a random token to .beads/obelisk.token with 0600 perms on startup, require clients to include it in every request). Even a simple token exchange eliminates trivial local exploitation.

Affected file: src/daemon.rs (lines 74-82, 147-188)
title: Add authentication to daemon TCP socket
